[JS Weird World] No.35 A Framework Reminder: Whitespace
Whitespace — the invisible characters that create space. Things like Enter, Tab, and Space. They help make our code more readable, and the engine doesn't actually execute them.
So we can take advantage of this whitespace behavior to do a few useful things. Since JavaScript is super relaxed about whitespace, we can drop comments in like this:
From the example above you can see we're free to use whitespace however we like in JavaScript, and doing this doesn't throw any errors.

So the takeaway is: make good use of this freedom to write comments. That way, when you come back to this code a few months or even a few years later, you can get your head around it again as fast as possible.

Whitespace — the invisible characters that create space. Things like Enter, Tab, and Space help make our code more readable, and they don't get executed by the engine. JavaScript is super relaxed about whitespace, which means we can use it to do some handy things, like dropping comments in unusual spots.
